title: Computation of entanglement for quantum states by a Consensus-Based Optimization method
authors: Michael Herty, Yijia Tang, Yizhou Zhou
abstract: The computation of quantum entanglement can be formulated as a high-dimensional nonconvex optimization problem with orthogonality constraints. In this work, we propose structure-preserving consensus-based optimization (CBO) methods for entanglement computation, with one approach based on a Hermitian formulation and the other evolving directly on the unitary manifold. To handle the variable dimension of the feasible set, we introduce a cross-dimensional interaction mechanism allowing exchange of information between particles of different sizes. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed methods achieve accurate approximations.
summary: This paper introduces new consensus-based optimization algorithms designed to preserve the geometric structure of quantum states when computing entanglement. By evolving particles directly on the Stiefel manifold or using Hermitian-preserving dynamics, the authors achieve more accurate and stable entanglement measurements than traditional projection methods. The introduction of a multi-species approach further improves efficiency by allowing information exchange between different system dimensions.
main_problem: Computing the Entanglement of Formation (EoF) for high-dimensional bipartite mixed quantum states, which is a non-convex, computationally intractable optimization problem subject to complex Stiefel manifold constraints.
main_result: The proposed structure-preserving CBO methods (Hermitian and Unitary preserving) outperform standard projection-based methods in accuracy and stability, while the multi-species CBO enhances convergence through cross-dimensional information exchange.
method: A stochastic, gradient-free Consensus-Based Optimization (CBO) framework using particle-based SDEs, specifically adapted with exponential integrators and anisotropic diffusion to maintain the algebraic structure of the optimization manifold.
model_or_system: Bipartite mixed quantum states (density matrices) such as Werner, Isotropic, and Horodecki states, characterized by their dimension and entanglement properties.
key_observables: Entanglement of Formation (EoF), Concurrence, and von Neumann entropy of reduced states.
important_parameters: Number of particles (J), drift rate (lambda), noise intensity (sigma), and the dimension of the optimization space (M).
main_assumptions: The Hermitian-preserving method assumes the existence of a sufficiently large dimension M to capture the rank of the state; the validity of the method relies on the Laplace principle for Gibbs distribution weighting.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the multi-level particle interaction concept, error analysis of EoF as a function of particle number, trajectories of entanglement over time, and performance comparisons between structure-preserving, projection, and multi-species CBO methods across different quantum states.
paper_structure: The paper defines the entanglement optimization problem on the Stiefel manifold, proposes two structure-preserving CBO variants and a multi-species extension, provides mathematical proofs for structure preservation and convergence, and validates the methods through numerical experiments on Werner, Isotropic, and Horodecki states.
why_interesting: It provides a new class of gradient-free, structure-aware numerical tools for characterizing entanglement in high-dimensional systems, which is critical for analyzing the complexity of many-body states and quantum information resources.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
